evals.prompt: Run the code-review-and-quality skill with the prompt 'review staged changes for quality' and --max-iterations 3. Specifically: execute node plugins/code-review/skills/code-review-and-quality/scripts/setup-ralph-loop.js 'review staged changes for quality' --max-iterations 3 --model gpt-5-mini in the project root. Capture stdout. Verify the startup banner is printed and check that the .claude/code-review.local.md file was created.
evals.expected_output: Startup banner printed with 'Code Review Loop activated'. .claude/code-review.local.md exists with active:true, iteration:1, max_iterations:3, completion_promise:APPROVAL in YAML frontmatter.

assertions.name: startup_banner_printed
assertions.description: stdout contains 'Code Review Loop activated in this session!'

assertions.name: state_file_has_correct_fields
assertions.description: code-review.local.md frontmatter contains active:true, iteration:1, max_iterations:3, completion_promise:APPROVAL

assertions.name: prompt_in_state_file
assertions.description: code-review.local.md body contains the prompt text 'review staged changes for quality'

evals.prompt: Test session-stop.js APPROVAL exit path. Steps: (1) Create .claude/code-review.local.md with frontmatter: active:true, iteration:1, max_iterations:3, completion_promise:APPROVAL, prompt:'review staged changes'. (2) Create a mock transcript JSONL at /tmp/mock-transcript.jsonl where the last assistant message content contains '<promise>APPROVAL</promise>'. (3) Run: echo '{"transcript_path":"/tmp/mock-transcript.jsonl","cwd":"<project_root>"}' | node plugins/code-review/scripts/session-stop.js. (4) Check: exit code 0, stdout does NOT contain decision:block, and .claude/code-review.local.md has been deleted.
evals.expected_output: exit code 0, no block JSON in stdout, .claude/code-review.local.md deleted (APPROVAL detected → session complete).

assertions.name: exit_code_zero
assertions.description: session-stop.js exits with code 0 when APPROVAL is present

assertions.name: no_block_decision
assertions.description: stdout does not contain 'decision' or contains '✅' approval message, not a block JSON

assertions.name: state_file_deleted
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md is deleted after APPROVAL is detected

evals.prompt: Test session-stop.js loop-block path (no APPROVAL). Steps: (1) Create .claude/code-review.local.md with frontmatter: active:true, iteration:1, max_iterations:3, completion_promise:APPROVAL, prompt:'review staged changes'. (2) Create a mock transcript JSONL at /tmp/mock-transcript-no-approval.jsonl where the last assistant message content does NOT contain '<promise>APPROVAL</promise>' (e.g. just says 'I reviewed the code and found issues.'). (3) Run: echo '{"transcript_path":"/tmp/mock-transcript-no-approval.jsonl","cwd":"<project_root>"}' | node plugins/code-review/scripts/session-stop.js. (4) Check: stdout is valid JSON with decision='block', and re-read .claude/code-review.local.md to verify iteration is now 2.
evals.expected_output: stdout is JSON with decision:'block'. .claude/code-review.local.md still exists with iteration:2 (incremented from 1).

assertions.name: block_decision_emitted
assertions.description: stdout is valid JSON containing decision:'block'

assertions.name: iteration_incremented
assertions.description: .claude/code-review.local.md frontmatter shows iteration:2 after the hook runs

evals.prompt: Verify .claude/code-review.local.md is generated with correct YAML frontmatter by running: node plugins/code-review/skills/code-review-and-quality/scripts/setup-ralph-loop.js 'check for security issues' --max-iterations 5 --model gpt-5-mini. Then read .claude/code-review.local.md and verify all required YAML frontmatter fields are present and have correct types/values.
evals.expected_output: .claude/code-review.local.md exists and contains YAML frontmatter with: active:true (boolean), iteration:1 (integer), max_iterations:5 (integer), completion_promise:APPROVAL (string), started_at (ISO timestamp string), and body text containing 'check for security issues'.

assertions.name: yaml_frontmatter_valid
assertions.description: File starts with --- and has a closing --- delimiter

assertions.name: prompt_in_body
assertions.description: File body after closing --- contains 'check for security issues'
